history.p: The Nuka-Cola Corporation is a beverage corporation founded and led by its CEO, John-Caleb Bradberton, best known for its flagship product, Nuka-Cola.

Nuka-Cola Corporation entered the beverage market in 2044 with the invention of  its iconic soft drink by chemist John-Caleb Bradberton after two years of experimentation. It took the U.S. market by storm, and in less than a year, stores were selling the soft drink nationwide. Its popularity grew rapidly, and within a few years, it became the most popular beverage in the country and eventually the world.

Inspired by the success of his brand, Bradberton decided to invest his business fortune in opening a theme park dedicated to the refreshing beverage. Nuka-World opened on May 1, 2050, less than a year after construction began. It initially opened with only two sections, Kiddie Kingdom and Nuka-Town U.S.A., but more were added as visitor numbers increased, reaching six sections by 2077, including Safari Adventure, Dry Rock Gulch, The World of Refreshment, and the Galactic Zone.
The constant drive to improve and innovate the formula led to numerous flavor variations, the most popular being Cherry, Grape, and Orange, launched just a few years after the corporation's founding.

The brand's popularity only grew over time. When the Nuka-Girl rocket costume was released in 2062, the outfits sold out immediately, and stores couldn't keep up with demand. By 2067, Nuka-Cola was ubiquitous, available in vending machines on nearly every street corner in the United States.
